title: smoked pork butt
directions: The night before smoking, clean the butt by removing the silver skin and place on a wire rack over a sheet tray. Place in the fridge overnight.

directions.step: The next day, preheat smoker to 225°F. Take pork out of the fridge and season generously with rub. Place the pork on the smoker fat side up. Add apple (or oak or mesquite) wood chips.

directions: Add wood chips as needed, every 30-45 minutes. Two hours in, add butter on top of the pork.
Keep smoking until a dark brown and black crust has formed on the outside of the pork (usually around 165°F internal temp). Spray with water every 60 minutes to keep moist.

directions.step: Once the bark has formed, you can wrap the pork tightly in butcher paper or heavy duty aluminum foil. Add the rest of your butter.

directions: Place the wrapped pork back on the smoker (or in the oven, there is no need to add more smoke at this stage).
Once the pork reaches an internal temp of 200°F, pull it off the smoker and let it rest for at least 1 hour.
When ready to eat, chop pork or shred with hands and serve.
notes: Cooking time varies by the size of the shoulder. Expect at least 1 hour/lb of meat plus 1 hour rest.
Use ~65g (≈ 37.5g/lb) salt. Will depend on the size and shape of the pork butt.
